title: Traffick Jamming
summary: "...[T]here are plenty of laws that require or authorize sanctions against governments that knowingly provide direct support to proliferators. But these laws offer little protection against those governments that—through neglect, corruption, or incompetence—fail to keep the materials and technology needed to make nuclear, biological, and chemical weapons from slipping out of their control and across their borders."
body: Overview Successful efforts to fight human trafficking can be a model for preventing the proliferation of nuclear, biological, and chemical weapons.
